title: Western disturbance brings rain, snow to parts of North India; wheat crops at risk
description: Unseasonal rain, hailstorms and fresh snowfall have swept across north India, disrupting normal life and raising serious concerns for farmers. Authorities have issued warnings about possible waterlogging, landslides and flash floods in vulnerable areas, while governments have begun assessing losses and assuring support to affected farmers.

In the Women's category, Indian players Vaishali Rameshbabu and Divya Deshmukh secured crucial wins, though their victories relied heavily on opponent errors. The Indian challenge in the Open section, led by Praggnanandhaa, lags significantly behind the leader.

Suspected ISIS operative arrested from Kushinagar in joint operation by UP ATS, Delhi Police
description: A suspected ISIS operative, Rizwan, was arrested in Kushinagar by UP ATS and Delhi Police. Previously jailed for terror activities and radicalizing youth, he allegedly reconnected with ISIS modules online after his release. Electronic devices seized are being examined for further leads.

This scarcity is impacting candidates' ability to connect with diverse voters, as these casual hangouts are crucial for reaching a cross-section of the electorate during election campaigns.

Giannis vs Bucks turns messy as NBA probes injury controversy
description: A major NBA dispute is unfolding involving the Milwaukee Bucks and star player Giannis Antetokounmpo. Antetokounmpo claims he is healthy, but the team states he is injured. This situation has sparked an NBA investigation into potential "tanking" by the Bucks to improve draft picks. Fans are siding with Antetokounmpo, raising concerns about his future with the team.

description: Tiger Woods faces more pressure after Jeromy Bullard hired lawyer Jason Fagnano following their Florida crash. While no injuries occurred, legal action may grow. Woods already faces DUI-related charges. His past accidents, possible rehab trip to Switzerland, and support from Vanessa Trump have all added new layers to an already serious situation, as reported by TMZ and Daily Mail.

title: Meta CTO’s message to engineers and college graduates looking to join technology industry
description: Meta's CTO, Andrew Bosworth, urged aspiring tech professionals to prioritize hands-on building over theoretical study. He emphasized immersing oneself in practical projects, from "vibe coding" with AI tools for software to using Raspberry Pi and Arduino for hardware.  Bosworth advocates for continuous experimentation and action to develop essential skills for the evolving tech landscape.

UP man killed serving in Russian army, kin allege coercive recruitment
description: A 22-year-old man from Rampur, serving in the Russian army, was killed in the Russia-Ukraine conflict. His family alleges he was coerced into military service after going abroad for work, with an audio clip suggesting his life was ruined. His body returned home, leaving his family devastated and seeking answers.

Amid job cuts, Canadian billionaire Kevin O’Leary points out a good thing about AI layoffs
description: Kevin O'Leary, the Canadian billionaire, asserts that artificial intelligence is elevating human creativity, making artists and storytellers more valuable than ever. He highlights how compelling social media content, especially short-form videos, now commands significant salaries due to measurable customer acquisition. O'Leary believes AI is a powerful productivity tool, fostering new industries and opportunities across all economic sectors.

title: Coach borrows Rs 10L to fire MP rural girls' team to national women's football league
description: Football coach Param Ashawar took a significant personal loan to fund his underprivileged girls' team, District Raisen Football Club, enabling their historic qualification for Tier 2 of the Indian Women's League. Despite financial burdens, the team's success, including a victory over Odisha FC, fuels his commitment to advancing women's football in Madhya Pradesh.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry join $150b mogul in heartwarming move
description: Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry have joined Warren Buffett in reviving his iconic charity lunch through a new auction titled “A Seat At The Table.” The initiative blends Buffett’s legacy in philanthropy with the Currys’ growing influence beyond basketball. With proceeds benefiting the Glide Foundation and Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation, the event aims to create meaningful change in underserved communities. Scheduled for June 24 in Omaha, the auction reflects a powerful collaboration focused on impact, purpose, and long-term community support.
